response.justification: Bogdan brings genuine Staff-level TypeScript/Node.js depth and is currently building MCP code generators and agentic workflow frameworks at re:cinq, which maps directly to the role's core delivery responsibilities. However, Python is entirely absent from the resume, and there is no mention of vector databases, RAG architectures, or multi-agent frameworks such as LangGraph or CrewAI. More critically, substantive AI/GenAI work appears limited to his current position started in April 2025, placing him far short of the mandatory 8-year AI solution-delivery requirement. His strong microservices, data-integration, and AWS background makes him worth a short exploratory call to assess undocumented AI depth.
strengths: Current hands-on work with Model Context Protocol (MCP) and agentic workflow infrastructure maps directly to the role's primary production-grade agentic-workflow responsibilities
Consistent Staff-level seniority across multiple companies with documented technical leadership, community education (workshops, talks), and open-source platform ownership
Proven TypeScript and AWS (CDK) production experience across high-throughput, data-intensive, microservices systems at scale
concerns: Python does not appear anywhere in the resume, yet it is listed as a core required language alongside TypeScript
No evidence of vector databases, RAG architectures, LLM evaluation pipelines, or multi-agent frameworks (LangGraph, CrewAI, or equivalents)
AI/GenAI experience is confined to the current role beginning April 2025, making the stated 8-year minimum for AI-driven solution delivery unmet by a wide margin
